steps.prompt: Which is the main finite verb?

hints.Desiderio: 'Desiderio' is a noun in the ablative meaning 'with desire'
hints.manducare: 'Manducare' is a complementary infinitive meaning 'to eat'
hints.pascha: 'Pascha' is a noun meaning 'Passover' — the direct object

hints.to despise: 'To despise' is 'despicere' — 'desideravi' comes from 'desiderare' (to long for)

steps.reveal: desideravi = I have desired (1st sing. perf.)

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'desidero' — 'desideravi' with -avi is perfect
hints.perfect subjunctive: Perfect subjunctive would be 'desideraverim' — 'desideravi' is indicative

hints.descendo, descendere, descendi, descensu: These are the parts of 'descendo' (to descend)
hints.despicio, despicere, despexi, despectum: These are the parts of 'despicio' (to look down on/despise)
hints.desero, deserere, deserui, desertum: These are the parts of 'desero' (to abandon/desert)

steps.target: cognate accusative / ablative of manner (Hebraism: intensifying figure)
choices: cognate accusative / ablative of manner (Hebraism: intensifying figure)
steps.reveal: Desiderio desideravi = With desire I have desired = I have earnestly desired

hints.ablative absolute: Ablative absolute requires a noun + participle — 'Desiderio' alone is not an ablative absolute
hints.indirect statement: Indirect statement uses accusative + infinitive — this is an intensifying Hebraism
hints.ablative of means: Ablative of means names an instrument — 'Desiderio' here intensifies the verb (figura etymologica)

hints.dative: Dative and ablative look the same for 2nd declension, but this is ablative of manner

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'desiderii' ends in -ii
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'desiderii' ends in -ii
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -us — 'desiderii' ends in -ii

hints.nominative: 'Pascha' is indeclinable; context (direct object of 'manducare') shows accusative function
hints.ablative: 'Pascha' is indeclinable — context determines it is the object to be eaten
hints.vocative: Vocative would be for direct address — 'pascha' is the object of 'manducare'

hints.mando, mandare, mandavi, mandatum: These are the parts of 'mando' (to command/entrust)
hints.mando, mandere, mandi, mansum: These are the parts of 'mando' (to chew) — 'manducare' is the frequentative form
hints.maneo, manere, mansi, mansum: These are the parts of 'maneo' (to remain)

hints.dative plural of 'vos': 'Vobis' alone is dative, but 'vobiscum' is the enclitic ablative + cum form
hints.nominative plural pronoun: Nominative plural would be 'vos' — 'vobiscum' is ablative with enclitic 'cum'
hints.accusative plural of 'vos': Accusative plural would be 'vos' — 'vobiscum' = cum + vobis (ablative)

translation: With desire I have desired to eat this Passover with you
